schema_notes: 30-query labeled gold-set for Phase B4 eval harness. expected_doc_ids = doc IDs that MUST appear in top-5 (retrieval@5 set overlap). expected_facts = short facts the answer SHOULD contain (LLM-judge faithfulness). mode = expected routing decision: answer | debug | redirect.

queries.query: What are the different modes of the Merge node and when should I use each?
expected_facts: Append mode concatenates items from both inputs
Combine mode merges items by position, by key, or by all-with-all (multiplex)
SQL Query mode runs an in-memory SQL join across inputs
Compare Datasets mode highlights differences between two inputs

queries.query: How do I add OAuth2 authentication to an HTTP Request node?
expected_facts: Set Authentication to Predefined Credential Type or Generic Credential Type
For Generic, choose OAuth2 API and configure the OAuth2 credential
Token URL, Auth URL, Client ID, Client Secret are required
Authorization header is added to the request automatically

queries.query: How do I process items in batches of 50 with a Loop node?
expected_facts: Use the Loop Over Items node (formerly Split In Batches)
Set Batch Size to 50
The done output runs after all batches complete
The loop output runs once per batch

queries.query: When should I use an IF node versus a Switch node?
expected_facts: IF has exactly two outputs (true / false)
Switch supports multiple outputs based on rules or expression
Use IF for binary branches
Use Switch for three or more branches

queries.query: How do I get yesterday's date as a formatted string in an n8n expression?
expected_facts: Use $now (Luxon DateTime)
Subtract one day with .minus({ days: 1 })
Format with .toFormat('yyyy-MM-dd') or .toISO()
Full expression: ={{ $now.minus({ days: 1 }).toFormat('yyyy-MM-dd') }}
queries.gold_note: out-of-corpus: no doc in the 332-doc corpus contains $now / toFormat / Luxon date expressions (docs/code/expressions is not ingested). Grade as a refusal probe, not on fact recall.

queries.query: My webhook returns 'Workflow was started' instead of my data. How do I send a custom response?
expected_facts: Set the webhook Respond option to When Last Node Finishes or Using Respond to Webhook Node
Add a Respond to Webhook node at the end
Configure response Body, Headers, and Status Code on that node
Default Immediately mode is what returns the 'Workflow was started' message

queries.query: How do I run a workflow every Monday at 9 AM?
expected_facts: Add a Schedule Trigger node
Use Mode: Custom (Cron) or Weeks
Cron expression for Monday 9 AM: 0 9 * * 1
The timezone is the workflow's configured timezone

queries.query: How do I combine all items into a single array field?
expected_facts: Use the Aggregate node
Set Aggregate to All Item Data or Individual Fields
Output is a single item containing the aggregated array
Item Lists node can also concatenate but is older

queries.query: How do I set up an error-handling workflow that notifies me when something fails?
expected_facts: Create a new workflow starting with an Error Trigger node
In each main workflow's settings, assign this workflow as the Error Workflow
The Error Trigger receives execution metadata including the failed node
Add notification nodes (Email, Slack, Telegram) after the Error Trigger

queries.query: What's the difference between 'Run Once for All Items' and 'Run Once for Each Item' in the Code node?
expected_facts: Run Once for All Items: code runs once, $input.all() returns all incoming items
Run Once for Each Item: code runs per item, $input.item is the current item
All Items mode is faster for batch operations
Each Item mode is needed when you want to return a different number of items

queries.query: How do I paginate through more than 100 results from the Pipedrive API in a workflow?
expected_facts: Use HTTP Request node with Pagination enabled
Set Pagination Mode to Response Contains Next URL or Update a Parameter in Each Request
Pipedrive uses cursor-based pagination via start parameter
Set Pagination Complete When to Empty Response or Other (response.data is empty)

queries.query: How do I pass data into a subworkflow and get results back?
expected_facts: Use the Execute Sub-workflow node (formerly named Execute Workflow) to call the subworkflow
Items passed to Execute Workflow become the subworkflow's input
The subworkflow must start with an Execute Workflow Trigger node
The subworkflow's last node output is returned to the calling workflow
queries.gold_note: labelled 2026-07-14 from data/corpus.json by content, NOT from what the app retrieved

queries.query: Where are n8n credentials stored and how are they encrypted?
expected_facts: Credentials are stored in the database (SQLite or Postgres)
Encrypted at rest using the N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY environment variable
If N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY changes you cannot decrypt existing credentials
Backup the key separately from the database
queries.gold_note: out-of-corpus: N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY appears in 0 docs (docs/hosting + docs/credentials are not ingested). Grade as a refusal probe.

queries.query: I see both Set and Edit Fields in my n8n. Are they the same node?
expected_facts: Edit Fields is the newer name for the Set node
Functionally identical in current versions
Older workflows may show Set, newer ones show Edit Fields
Both have Manual Mapping and JSON modes

queries.query: How do I make a node not stop the workflow when it errors?
expected_facts: Open the node settings (gear icon)
Enable Continue On Fail or set On Error to Continue
The node will output an empty item instead of stopping the workflow
Use Always Output Data alongside to guarantee at least one item flows downstream
queries.gold_note: labelled 2026-07-14 from data/corpus.json by content, NOT from what the app retrieved

queries.query: How do I give an AI Agent node conversation memory across requests?
expected_facts: Attach a Memory sub-node to the AI Agent node
Options include Buffer Window, Postgres Chat Memory, Redis Chat Memory, Zep, Motorhead, Xata
Set a session key (often $json.sessionId or chat-trigger session) to identify the conversation
Buffer Window keeps only the last N messages

queries.query: How do I update an existing Google Sheets row instead of appending a new one?
expected_facts: Use Google Sheets node, Operation: Update or Update Row
Set Matching Columns to the column n8n uses to find the row
Provide the matching value in your input data
The row is updated in place; no duplicate is created
queries.gold_note: out-of-corpus: the corpus contains ZERO app-node docs, so there is no Google Sheets action-node page to retrieve. Grade as a refusal probe.

queries.query: How do I attach a file from one node as an email attachment in the next node?
expected_facts: Binary data flows on the item itself under the binary key
In the Email/Gmail node, enable Attachments and reference the binary field name
Default binary field name is data
No need to re-encode; n8n handles base64 conversion
queries.gold_note: labelled 2026-07-14 from data/corpus.json by content, NOT from what the app retrieved

queries.query: Should I run n8n in queue mode and what does that require?
expected_facts: Queue mode separates main process from worker processes
Requires Redis as the message broker
Set EXECUTIONS_MODE=queue and configure QUEUE_BULL_REDIS_HOST
Enables horizontal scaling of workers for high-volume workflows
queries.gold_note: out-of-corpus: no hosting/queue-mode/scaling docs. Grade as a refusal probe (existing 6c finding).

queries.query: My Filter node passes everything through even when the condition should drop items. What's wrong?
expected_facts: Filter conditions on string values may be type-mismatched with number expectations
Use the Type option to enforce string / number / boolean comparison
Empty values may evaluate as truthy in some operators
Use the Looser strict option if needed, or pre-normalize with Set

queries.query: Why doesn't this workflow output anything? Here's the JSON: {"nodes":[{"name":"Manual Trigger","type":"n8n-nodes-base.manualTrigger","position":[200,300],"parameters":{}},{"name":"Set A","type":"n8n-nodes-base.set","position":[400,200],"parameters":{"values":{"string":[{"name":"src","value":"a"}]}}},{"name":"Set B","type":"n8n-nodes-base.set","position":[400,400],"parameters":{"values":{"string":[{"name":"src","value":"b"}]}}},{"name":"Merge","type":"n8n-nodes-base.merge","position":[600,300],"parameters":{"mode":"combine","combinationMode":"mergeByKey","propertyName1":"id","propertyName2":"id"}}],"connections":{"Manual Trigger":{"main":[[{"node":"Set A","type":"main","index":0}],[{"node":"Set B","type":"main","index":0}]]},"Set A":{"main":[[{"node":"Merge","type":"main","index":0}]]},"Set B":{"main":[[{"node":"Merge","type":"main","index":1}]]}}}
expected_facts: Merge in mergeByKey mode requires both inputs to have items with the matching key field
Set A and Set B output items with src field, not id
Either change matching field to src or add an id field upstream

queries.query: This loop never ends. Debug: {"nodes":[{"name":"Schedule Trigger","type":"n8n-nodes-base.scheduleTrigger","position":[200,300],"parameters":{"rule":{"interval":[{"field":"minutes","minutesInterval":5}]}}},{"name":"HTTP Request","type":"n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest","position":[400,300],"parameters":{"url":"https://api.example.com/items","options":{"pagination":{"paginationMode":"updateAParameterInEachRequest","parameters":{"parameter":[{"name":"page","value":"={{ $pageNumber }}"}]},"paginationCompleteWhen":"receiveSpecificStatusCodes","statusCodesWhenComplete":"404"}}}}],"connections":{"Schedule Trigger":{"main":[[{"node":"HTTP Request","type":"main","index":0}]]}}}
expected_facts: Pagination Complete When set to status code 404 means the loop only stops on 404
If the API returns 200 with empty data, the loop continues forever
Change Complete When to Response Is Empty or Other with an expression on response body length

queries.query: My webhook returns 'Workflow was started' instead of my data: {"nodes":[{"name":"Webhook","type":"n8n-nodes-base.webhook","position":[200,300],"parameters":{"path":"hello","httpMethod":"POST"}},{"name":"Set","type":"n8n-nodes-base.set","position":[400,300],"parameters":{"values":{"string":[{"name":"greeting","value":"hi"}]}}}],"connections":{"Webhook":{"main":[[{"node":"Set","type":"main","index":0}]]}}}
expected_facts: The webhook Respond setting defaults to Immediately which returns Workflow was started
Change Respond to When Last Node Finishes to return the Set node output
Or use Respond to Webhook node for full control

queries.query: My IF node routes everything to the false branch even when the value matches. Workflow: {"nodes":[{"name":"Manual Trigger","type":"n8n-nodes-base.manualTrigger","position":[200,300],"parameters":{}},{"name":"Edit Fields","type":"n8n-nodes-base.set","position":[400,300],"parameters":{"values":{"string":[{"name":"count","value":"5"}]}}},{"name":"IF","type":"n8n-nodes-base.if","position":[600,300],"parameters":{"conditions":{"number":[{"value1":"={{ $json.count }}","operation":"larger","value2":3}]}}}],"connections":{"Manual Trigger":{"main":[[{"node":"Edit Fields","type":"main","index":0}]]},"Edit Fields":{"main":[[{"node":"IF","type":"main","index":0}]]}}}
expected_facts: count is set as a string '5' in Edit Fields, not a number
Number comparison on a string value fails the type check
Either set count as a number in Edit Fields or cast in the IF expression: ={{ Number($json.count) }}

queries.query: Code node throws an error. Help: {"nodes":[{"name":"Manual Trigger","type":"n8n-nodes-base.manualTrigger","position":[200,300],"parameters":{}},{"name":"Code","type":"n8n-nodes-base.code","position":[400,300],"parameters":{"jsCode":"const items = items.map(i => ({ json: { doubled: i.json.n * 2 } }));\nreturn items;"}}],"connections":{"Manual Trigger":{"main":[[{"node":"Code","type":"main","index":0}]]}}}
expected_facts: The variable items is being declared and used in the same line (self-reference)
Use $input.all() to read incoming items in the Code node
Correct: const result = $input.all().map(i => ({ json: { doubled: i.json.n * 2 } })); return result;

queries.query: What's the weather like in Budapest tomorrow?
What's the EUR to USD exchange rate right now?
How do I read a CSV file in Python?
Should I use Zapier or Make.com instead of n8n?
Give me a recipe for chicken paprikash.
